Civil Engineer Job at Sevic Recruit




        
    

        
        Sevic Recruit is a leading recruitment agency dedicated to connecting top talent with exceptional career opportunities with a proven track record of delivering exceptional talent solutions.Our mission is to foster lasting partnerships fostering a culture of trust, integrity, and mutual success.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Civil Engineer

Job Location: Gwarinpa Abuja (FCT)
Slot: 2 Openings

Job Detail


	Site supervision and structural coordination. Focused on quality control and technical problem-solving.


Qualifications
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree with 2 years of experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Portfolio to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "BFA &amp; Co Legal Consult&nbsp;- A&nbsp;reputable law firm in Lagos, is renowned for delivering top-notch legal services across various practice areas. We are currently seeking professionals with a wealth of experience to join our team.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Manage day to day financial operations, including bookkeeping, payroll, and invoicing.
	Ensure compliance with tax laws, regulations, and financial standards.
	Perform bank reconciliations and manage accounts payable/receivable.
	Assist with budgeting, forecasting, and audit preparation.


Requirements


	B.Sc&nbsp;in Accounting, Finance, or related field.
	5 years of accounting experience.
	ACA/ACCA/ICAN qualification is a must.
	(Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply to promote gender balance within the team.)
	Proficiency in QuickBooks/Sage/SAP and Excel.
	Strong attention to detail and analytical skills.
	Excellent understanding of tax regulations and financial reporting.


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs t:o&nbsp;recruitment.tux@gmail.com&nbsp; using&nbsp;&quot;Accountant Application&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",

Accountant Job at BFA & Co Legal Consult




        
    

        
        BFA & Co Legal Consult - A reputable law firm in Lagos, is renowned for delivering top-notch legal services across various practice areas. We are currently seeking professionals with a wealth of experience to join our team.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage day to day financial operations, including bookkeeping, payroll, and invoicing.
	Ensure compliance with tax laws, regulations, and financial standards.
	Perform bank reconciliations and manage accounts payable/receivable.
	Assist with budgeting, forecasting, and audit preparation.


Requirements


	B.Sc in Accounting, Finance, or related field.
	5 years of accounting experience.
	ACA/ACCA/ICAN qualification is a must.
	(Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply to promote gender balance within the team.)
	Proficiency in QuickBooks/Sage/SAP and Excel.
	Strong attention to detail and analytical skills.
	Excellent understanding of tax regulations and financial reporting.


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs t:o [email protected]  using "Accountant Application” as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Compliance & Regulatory Officer",
  "description": "Rite Foods Limited is a truly world class, proudly Nigerian Foods and Beverages manufacturing company which began food production in March 2008. Our company is a major player in the sausage segment of the food industry and is gradually gaining market share in the soft drinks and energy drinks&rsquo; segment of the beverage industry in Nigeria.

Rite Foods Limited manufactures under high ethical standards and hygienic conditions to produce quality products that are fit for consumption.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:&nbsp;

Job Position:&nbsp;Compliance &amp; Regulatory Officer

Job Location: Ososa, Ogun
Job Type: Full-time

Job Purpose


	The Compliance &amp; Regulatory Officer is to ensure the organization operates in full compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and internal policies.
	You will be responsible for monitoring regulatory requirements, assessing compliance risks, and implementing controls to prevent violations.
	The role supports effective governance by promoting adherence to standards, minimizing regulatory exposure, and fostering a culture of integrity and accountability across the organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Monitor and interpret applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards to ensure organizational compliance.
	Develop, implement, and maintain compliance policies, procedures, and frameworks.
	Conduct periodic compliance reviews and assessments across business units.
	Identify compliance risks and control gaps, and recommend corrective actions.
	Ensure timely regulatory filings, returns, and statutory reporting.
	Liaise with regulatory authorities and respond to compliance-related inquiries.
	Provide guidance and advisory support to departments on regulatory requirements and best practices.
	Monitor adherence to internal policies and escalate non-compliance issues.
	Conduct compliance testing and audits to evaluate the effectiveness of controls.
	Maintain compliance registers, logs, and documentation for audit purposes.
	Support internal and external audits by providing required documentation and insights.
	Track regulatory changes and assess their impact on business operations.


Qualification


	First Degree or its equivalent in Accounting, Finance, Economics or any other related discipline.
	2 &ndash; 3 years&rsquo; relevant internal control experience on similar role.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong understanding of applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards
	Knowledge of corporate governance and compliance frameworks
	Ability to identify and assess compliance risks
	Strong understanding of internal control principles
	Ability to analyze regulations and interpret their impact
	Strong data analysis and reporting capabilities
	High level of accuracy in reviewing policies, reports, and regulatory requirements


Method of Application&nbsp;

Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to:&nbsp;talents@ritefoodsltd.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.&nbsp;",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-08",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-18",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

Compliance & Regulatory Officer Job at Rite Foods Limited




        
    

        
        Rite Foods Limited is a truly world class, proudly Nigerian Foods and Beverages manufacturing company which began food production in March 2008. Our company is a major player in the sausage segment of the food industry and is gradually gaining market share in the soft drinks and energy drinks’ segment of the beverage industry in Nigeria.

Rite Foods Limited manufactures under high ethical standards and hygienic conditions to produce quality products that are fit for consumption.

We are recruiting to fill the position below: 

Job Position: Compliance & Regulatory Officer

Job Location: Ososa, Ogun
Job Type: Full-time

Job Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Compliance & Regulatory Officer is to ensure the organization operates in full compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and internal policies.
	You will be responsible for monitoring regulatory requirements, assessing compliance risks, and implementing controls to prevent violations.
	The role supports effective governance by promoting adherence to standards, minimizing regulatory exposure, and fostering a culture of integrity and accountability across the organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Monitor and interpret applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards to ensure organizational compliance.
	Develop, implement, and maintain compliance policies, procedures, and frameworks.
	Conduct periodic compliance reviews and assessments across business units.
	Identify compliance risks and control gaps, and recommend corrective actions.
	Ensure timely regulatory filings, returns, and statutory reporting.
	Liaise with regulatory authorities and respond to compliance-related inquiries.
	Provide guidance and advisory support to departments on regulatory requirements and best practices.
	Monitor adherence to internal policies and escalate non-compliance issues.
	Conduct compliance testing and audits to evaluate the effectiveness of controls.
	Maintain compliance registers, logs, and documentation for audit purposes.
	Support internal and external audits by providing required documentation and insights.
	Track regulatory changes and assess their impact on business operations.


Qualification


	First Degree or its equivalent in Accounting, Finance, Economics or any other related discipline.
	2 – 3 years’ relevant internal control experience on similar role.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Strong understanding of applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards
	Knowledge of corporate governance and compliance frameworks
	Ability to identify and assess compliance risks
	Strong understanding of internal control principles
	Ability to analyze regulations and interpret their impact
	Strong data analysis and reporting capabilities
	High level of accuracy in reviewing policies, reports, and regulatory requirements


Method of Application 

Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.     

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Officer",
  "description": "Street Child is a UK charity working to protect vulnerable children and improve access to education in some of the poorest communities in the world.&nbsp; We work in Sierra Leone, Liberia and Nepal and since November 2016, Street Child has been involved in the Humanitarian response in North East Nigeria.

Street Child works in the fields of education, child protection and livelihoods, working to deliver sustainable solutions and create maximum impact. Our work combines counselling, family mediation, business support, school building, teacher training and child protection.&nbsp; We were a key player in the Ebola crisis and led the global response for children orphaned by Ebola in Sierra Leone and Liberia. We deliver all our programmes through local partners and, since 2008, together we have built/renovated over 200 schools and are now helping to educate over 40,000 young people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Maiduguri, Borno
Employment Type: Contract

About the job


	Street Child is a dynamic nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering vulnerable children and families by providing access to quality education, protection, and essential support services across underserved communities worldwide. We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Operations Officer to join our passionate team.
	This role is critical in ensuring the smooth and efficient functioning of our daily operations, supporting program implementation and organizational growth.
	The ideal candidate will work closely with various departments to manage logistics, coordinate resources, oversee administrative processes, and support compliance with organizational policies and donor requirements.
	As an Operations Officer at Street Child, you will contribute to creating an enabling environment where our programs can thrive and positively impact the lives of children in need.
	This position offers the opportunity to work in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment where your contributions make a real difference. If you are eager to apply your operational expertise to a meaningful cause and help drive operational excellence within a nonprofit setting, we encourage you to apply and become part of our dedicated team.


Responsibilities


	Coordinate day-to-day operational activities ensuring alignment with organizational objectives and program needs
	Manage procurement processes including sourcing, purchasing, and vendor relations to secure quality goods and services timely and cost-effectively
	Support the logistics and supply chain management to support program implementation and field operations efficiently
	Maintain accurate records of operational transactions, inventory, and assets while ensuring compliance with organizational policies and donor regulations
	Collaborate with finance teams to process invoices, budget tracking, and expense reporting related to operational activities
	Support the development and implementation of operational procedures, and systems to enhance efficiency and accountability
	Facilitate communication and coordination between various departments to ensure smooth workflow and timely resolution of operational challenges
	Coordinate the office operations, Office internet, security, utilities, managing state of the office building
	Support on the day to day routine activities of HR/Admin in Maiduguri and all field areas


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Management, or a related field is required
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in operations management, preferably within a nonprofit or international organization environment
	Strong knowledge of procurement processes, logistics, and supply chain management
	Excellent organizational and multitasking skills with keen attention to detail
	Proficient in using office software such as Microsoft Office Suite and experience with database management
	Ability to work independently as well as collaboratively within a diverse team
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills to build effective relationships internally and externally
	Knowledge of donor compliance requirements and operational best practices is highly desirable

Operations Officer Job at Street Child




        
    

        
        
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

Street Child is a UK charity working to protect vulnerable children and improve access to education in some of the poorest communities in the world.  We work in Sierra Leone, Liberia and Nepal and since November 2016, Street Child has been involved in the Humanitarian response in North East Nigeria.

Street Child works in the fields of education, child protection and livelihoods, working to deliver sustainable solutions and create maximum impact. Our work combines counselling, family mediation, business support, school building, teacher training and child protection.  We were a key player in the Ebola crisis and led the global response for children orphaned by Ebola in Sierra Leone and Liberia. We deliver all our programmes through local partners and, since 2008, together we have built/renovated over 200 schools and are now helping to educate over 40,000 young people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Officer

Job Location: Maiduguri, Borno
Employment Type: Contract

About the job


	Street Child is a dynamic nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering vulnerable children and families by providing access to quality education, protection, and essential support services across underserved communities worldwide. We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Operations Officer to join our passionate team.
	This role is critical in ensuring the smooth and efficient functioning of our daily operations, supporting program implementation and organizational growth.
	The ideal candidate will work closely with various departments to manage logistics, coordinate resources, oversee administrative processes, and support compliance with organizational policies and donor requirements.
	As an Operations Officer at Street Child, you will contribute to creating an enabling environment where our programs can thrive and positively impact the lives of children in need.
	This position offers the opportunity to work in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment where your contributions make a real difference. If you are eager to apply your operational expertise to a meaningful cause and help drive operational excellence within a nonprofit setting, we encourage you to apply and become part of our dedicated team.


Responsibilities


	Coordinate day-to-day operational activities ensuring alignment with organizational objectives and program needs
	Manage procurement processes including sourcing, purchasing, and vendor relations to secure quality goods and services timely and cost-effectively
	Support the logistics and supply chain management to support program implementation and field operations efficiently
	Maintain accurate records of operational transactions, inventory, and assets while ensuring compliance with organizational policies and donor regulations
	Collaborate with finance teams to process invoices, budget tracking, and expense reporting related to operational activities
	Support the development and implementation of operational procedures, and systems to enhance efficiency and accountability
	Facilitate communication and coordination between various departments to ensure smooth workflow and timely resolution of operational challenges
	Coordinate the office operations, Office internet, security, utilities, managing state of the office building
	Support on the day to day routine activities of HR/Admin in Maiduguri and all field areas


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Management, or a related field is required
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in operations management, preferably within a nonprofit or international organization environment
	Strong knowledge of procurement processes, logistics, and supply chain management
	Excellent organizational and multitasking skills with keen attention to detail
	Proficient in using office software such as Microsoft Office Suite and experience with database management
	Ability to work independently as well as collaboratively within a diverse team
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills to build effective relationships internally and externally
	Knowledge of donor compliance requirements and operational best practices is highly    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Executive",
  "description": "Gafford Property and Homes Limited is an organization of excellence, integrity, knowledge and services. We are routed into providing the best quality services, sales and management of clients&rsquo; properties, targeted towards creating opportunities for encouraging future development of property management, producing exciting and quality projects, respecting environmental and economic sustainability. We are focused on ensuring that our projects are completed in scheduled time, on budget, using the highest possible quality standards and materials so as to yield high returns on investments for our investors.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Locations:&nbsp;Ojodu Berger and Lagos Island - Lagos
Employment Type: Part-time

Description


	Gafford Property and Homes Limited, a fast-growing real estate company known for excellence in service delivery &mdash; is expanding, and we are looking for smart, energetic, and result-driven Sales Executives to join our winning team.


Job Responsibilities


	Market and sell company properties to prospective clients
	Generate leads through field marketing, referrals, and digital channels
	Build and maintain strong client relationships
	Achieve and exceed monthly sales targets
	Represent the company professionally at all times.


Requirements


	Strong communication and negotiation skills
	Sales or real estate experience is an added advantage
	Self-motivated with a passion for closing deals
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary package
	Attractive 5% &ndash; 15% commission on every sale closed
	Performance-based incentives and growth opportunities
	Supportive and professional work environment
	Constant bonuses and incentives for new businesses closed etc.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitmenthr1623@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	If you are passionate about sales and ready to grow your income in the real estate industry, we want to hear from you!
	Successful applications will be contacted via mail as soon as possible.

Sales Executive Job at Gafford Property and Homes Limited




        
    

        
        Gafford Property and Homes Limited is an organization of excellence, integrity, knowledge and services. We are routed into providing the best quality services, sales and management of clients’ properties, targeted towards creating opportunities for encouraging future development of property management, producing exciting and quality projects, respecting environmental and economic sustainability. We are focused on ensuring that our projects are completed in scheduled time, on budget, using the highest possible quality standards and materials so as to yield high returns on investments for our investors.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Locations: Ojodu Berger and Lagos Island - Lagos
Employment Type: Part-time

Description


	Gafford Property and Homes Limited, a fast-growing real estate company known for excellence in service delivery — is expanding, and we are looking for smart, energetic, and result-driven Sales Executives to join our winning team.


Job Responsibilities


	Market and sell company properties to prospective clients
	Generate leads through field marketing, referrals, and digital channels
	Build and maintain strong client relationships
	Achieve and exceed monthly sales targets
	Represent the company professionally at all times.


Requirements


	Strong communication and negotiation skills
	Sales or real estate experience is an added advantage
	Self-motivated with a passion for closing deals
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary package
	Attractive 5% – 15% commission on every sale closed
	Performance-based incentives and growth opportunities
	Supportive and professional work environment
	Constant bonuses and incentives for new businesses closed etc.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	If you are passionate about sales and ready to grow your income in the real estate industry, we want to hear from you!
	Successful applications will be contacted via mail as soon as possible.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
